CHAP. Xxix . the patient hypocrite be he that set a demure countenance upon a froward heart . Defin. patience be a virtue of that use and necessity , that , without it , no man can possess himself , although he shall be possessor of the whole world : by it , a christian possess his soul luke 21.19 with comfort , when the world have leave he nothing but persecution and misery for his portion . therefore , as the christian endeavour to store himself with this grace , as hav•ng more daily use of this , in reference to the world , than of any other , ( except faith , which keep life in his patience ; ) so the hypocrite strive to be the true christian ape in this , as well as in other endowment . yea , in this he more serious labour , because his own discretion and experience can but tell he , that there be no live without it ; unless so , as death will be choose rather than life Jer. 8.3 . but as we see in plant , fruit and herb , there be a vast difference between such as industry , care and art have plant , or sow in garden , and those that be wild , and grow natural in field or wood , without any such culture or cost bestow upon they ; which make they upon the matter , ofttimes , differre specie , to differ in kind from the other : so be it here . the hypocrite patience be of another kind , a mere counterfeit that the spirit of Christ never plant ; a kind of wild patience , like to the true , in some outward show , as hemlock be to parsley ; but , proceed from , and serve only to cover a froward heart , which , early or late , be find lodge in the bosom of every hypocrite , of every evil doer Prov. 3.32 Psal. 18.26 . this froward heart , void both of righteousness and true holiness , the hypocrite endeavour to cover with a vizard of patience : where patience may commend he unto man , or promote some other design of his own . and whereas impatiency and anger discover themselves , for the most part , by the fierceness , or discontent of the countenance , and look ; this hypocrite seek by all mean to cover his inward fret and forwardness , by put on a serene and friendly countenance thereupon , as Cain do upon his inward discontent and rancour against his brother , till he have get he into the field , where he fall upon he , and slay he Gen. 4.8 . but , as he that hide hatred with lie lip Prov. 10.18 , and lay up deceit within he , when he speak fair , be not to be believe , for there be seven abomination in his heart , which wickedness God will one day discover , and cause to be show before the whole congregation Prov. 26.24 etc ; so this hypocrite shall be unmask as Haman be , notwithstanding all his courtly carriage and compliment towards Esther the queen , who he have destine to death as well as Mordecai , at who he conceive so much indignation , that no less than the destruction of the whole nation of the Jew be able to pacify it . yet he carry it smooth , accept of the queen invitation , and after glory in it Esth. 5.13 , that none can suspect the least discontent to lurk in his bosom ; so exact do he refrain himself , and temper his countenance , till his pride and inward rage and fury grow ripe for a discovery , that soon end in his own fall and confusion . differ . on the contrary , the true Christian be patient in heart , and endeavour to let patience have her perfect work there . he have bring his heart to take up the yoke of Jesus Christ Mat 11.29 , which yoke be by Christ himself declare to be his meekness and lowliness of heart , which ever produce patience , as the genuine daughter of both the former . he be meek , that be of a quiet spirit . therefore these be join together baptize Pet. 3.4 . he be meek , that be , calm , without storm within , as well as without . there be a calmness in his heart , as well as in his countenance ; meekness be a moderation of anger , therefore near of kin to patience . all the difference between they be but this ; meekness be a moderation of anger , patience be a subdue of it : patience make a man quiet to stand under the burden lay upon he , meekness make he not only quiet in suffer , but tractable to learn of God , and to follow God , even while he suffer . patience allay wrath ; meekness prevent it . this lesson he have learn of his master : not to colour , or cover ; but , to cure his passion , and to cast out that unruly beast of anger raise in his breast ; his discretion , put he on to exercise this grace of patience . for , the discretion of man ( say Solomon ) defer his anger Prov. 19 11 , not only for a time , as watch a better opportunity , as Esau do for kill his brother Jacob Gen. 27.41 : but , for ever , as look to God who appoint , rather than man that inflict that trouble and sorrow which exercise his patience ; resolve to bear the indignation of the lord Mic. 7.9 . and he look to man the instrument , with pity rather than revenge . for , say his lord before he , father , forgive they , for they know not what they do luke 23.34 . by patience , he bring forth fruit , to wit , obedience to the word luke 8.15 : by this , in the sharp persecution , he possess his soul , even while other torture his body luke 21.19 . this be the patience of the saint , even of they that keep the commandment of God , and the faith of Jesus rev. 14.12 . thus , this hypocrite put on patience for his upper garment , as Tamar cover her self with a veil , that she may conceal who she be Gen. 38.14 . the true christian take in patience as a cordial to his heart , and be careful to get it to work kind within , before he endeavour to show patience without : the patience of the one dissemble anger ; Deor. l. 2. c . 1. by Jove they understand dominatorem rerum and omnia nutu regentem , and praesentem ac praepotentem deum : which be a full description of god infinite power and presence and government of the world . when we call Jupiter Opt. Max. and Salutaris , and Hospitalis and Stator , de finib . Bon. and Mal. l. 3. c . 20. we mean , say Tully , that the safety of man depend upon his protection . and that they give he the title of Opt. Max. to express his power and goodness ; pro domo sva . c . 57 but first Opt. then Max. because it be a great thing to do good , de Nat. Deor. l. 2. c . 25. than to exercise power . you may safe , say Seneca , call God by the title of Jupiter Opt. Max. and tonans and Stator , Seneca de benefice . l. 4. c . 7. not from stop the roman army , but because all thing do stand by he . and you may give he what name you please , while you thereby express his divine power and efficacy , as libre parens because he be the author of all thing , Hercules because of his irresistible force , Mercury for his wisdom . if you have receive a kindness from Seneca , and you shall say you owe it to Annaeus , or Lucius , you will not change the person but his name : for what name soever you call he by , he be the same person still ; you may use what name you please , while you mean the same thing . and lest we shall think this only a philosophical subtlety in Seneca , Senec. but the main thing be this ; when God be angry with Adam , the man fall from the state of grace ; for God withdraw his grace , and we return to the state of mere nature , of our prime creation . and although i be not of Petrus Diaconus his mind , who say , that when we all fall in Adam , we fall into the dirt , and not only so , but we fall also upon a heap of stone ; London , print for Giles Calvert , and be to be sell at the black-spread-eagle , at the west end of Paul . 1650. to all the several society of people , call church , in the presbyterian , independent , or any other form of profession , in the service of God . brethren , and fellow-member of mankind ; this follow declaration of the word of life be a free gift to i from the father himself ; and i receive it not from man ; when i have write it , i be move to send it to you immediate ; but i delay it by almost a fortnight , and think not of it ; then one night as i wake out of sleep , the voice be in my very heart and mouth , ready to come forth ; go send it to the church ; thereupon i be fill with great love to you , my heart pant with love towards you , pity your condition ; in that there be a great strive , as it be for life among you , and yet you lie under the power of death and bondage , and know not , or at least do not actual hold forth that you know , that spirit which in word you seem to profess . you speak and preach of the life of love ; but you have not the power of it ; your verbal profession , without the pure righteous action , show you general to be outlandish man , of several nation , under the government of darkness , and that you be not yet the true inhabitant of the land of love . before you live you must die , and before you be bind up into one universal body , all your particular body and society must be tear to piece ; for the true light be come now once more , not only to shake the earth , that be , Moses worship , but heaven also ; that which you call gospel-worship , and the kingdom without shall fall , that so the kingdom within may be establish ; for all your particular church be like the enclosure of land which hedge in some to be heir of life , and hedge out other ; one say Christ be here with they ; another saying no : but he be there with they ; but true brethren , you shall see and find , that Christ who be the universal power of love be not confine to party , or private chamber ; but he be the power of life , light , and truth , now rise up to fill the earth , mankind with himself . well , i have obey the voice ; and i have send this to you , with a heart free and full of love towards you ; some of you will receive this with gentleness ; other will be offend ; to some it will be refresh , but other will storm , and prepare war against it ; and the Ishmaelites they will scoff ; but be it so : yet my armour be try , i be sure it will keep off the blow . you shall find i speak of the garden of Eden , which be the spirit of man , not the spirit of beast . and in that garden there be weed and herb . the weed be these . selfe● , pride , envy ; covetousness after riches , honour , pleasure , imagination , think he can live in peace , unless he enjoy this or that outward object ; and sometime the joy of envy when he obtain his end ; and sometime the sorrow of covetousness when he be cross , rule as king in the garden . and the stink weed overspreads the sweet flower and herb , which be the light of the spirit of truth . there be likewise the most venomous weed call hypocrisy , attend with evil surmise , grudge , speak and promise , nay , swear one thing , yet do another , invent much show of holiness to compass his selfish end ; and while he rule king , as he do rule at this day ; he be assist with vain glory , fear of be disthron , opp•essing other , unmerciful ; careless of former promise and engagement ; persecute those who do such thing as he promise and vow to do , while he be a servant , and such like . and as you may call those by the name of weed ; so likewise you may call they outlandish man ; for they be not the true native inhabitant of the heart , but stranger to the righreous spirit . and all these with their fellow make up but one power of darkness , devil , or father of lie . and this power be the night time of mankind , or the absence of the son of righteousness from the heart . this power be the reprobate which the word of life have reject , this shall never enter into true rest ; this be the wicked man , or dragon in you , which cause all war and sortowe ; and the son that cause shame ; the son of bondage , which must not abide in the heart for ever , but must be cast out . therefore , so long as you labour under this selfish , dark , imaginary power , you be stranger to the son of righteousness ; if this dark power bear sway in you ; it be he who you profess , and it be he who you call God , and Christ ; for that power that you hold forth in your action , be the spirit you profess ; if your action be full of self love , as you may know it by your hasty anger , when your religion be question , and by your snappish bitterness against those that differ from you ; then this dark power be he you worship . there be likewise in the garden of Eden ( man heart ) sweet flower and herb ; as joy , peace , love , humility , self-denial , patience , sincerity , truth , or equity . these be the true inhabitant in the righteous land ; and all these make up but one power , or body , which be the seed or tree of life in you . and this power be the daytime of mankind , or the presence of the son of righteousness in the heart ; this power be the elect , the son of the father in who he delight ; this son shall live for ever in rest , peace , and in the power of eternal life ; this be the righteous man , this be Michael , the seed Christ , or blessing .

methinks if man have any sense of religion , they ought to sigh continual , for the deplorable condition of the church , and of the Greeks and protestant , who we have cast headlong into the evil they now labour under . some people will have it , that because the greek patriarch among themselves hold that place which the council of nice , and the emperor Constantine give to all patriarch , he of roam , who have the first place , ought still to keep it ; and as in place he be the first bishop , and the only patriarch in the west , he ought still to enjoy these prerogative . but first of all , none of the greek patriarch , unless it be that John of Constantinople against who saint Gregory write so vehement , ever pretend to bear rule over the other bishop , nor over the church , much less over Christian prince , as the pope do ; and the patriarch of roam for above three hundred year after his institution , never attempt it . second , the place which the bishop of roam hold , be propter principalitatem vrbis , in regard of the dignity of the city , which now have no weight at all , roam be no long the seat of the empire , but the sink and common-shore of all filthy iniquity , a den of thief , and a nest of Satan , Nido di Satanazzo , and the very habitation of Slowth , laziness and beggary . that be to say , that great man be not to be suffer in a nation ; and that be all miserable , they will make no commotion : whereas the equality which i speak of , will produce not only a firm and last peace , but also the abundance of all spiritual and temporal good . there be also some people who pretend , that if we acknowledge a necessity of have archbishop and primate , who take their place above bishop institute by Jesus Christ , though the dignity of archbishop or primate be not so ; or else every patriarch in his turn . thus be the church ancient govern without tyranny ; by this mean do religion spread it self abroad with great success in all country , and not by a pretend bishop , who be a worldly prince , and have ruin the church . we see , that heretofore , among the pagan , king have be sacrificer and minister of religion . among the Jew also , at the beginning , we find , that the head of family , who be sovereign , do take upon they the offer of sacrifice , and perform divine service ;
